citations.note: worldwide, royalty-free, non-sublicensable, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to exercise the Licensed Rights; no non-commercial restriction
reproduce and Share the Licensed Material, in whole or in part
produce and reproduce, but not Share, Adapted Material
For the avoidance of doubt, You do not have permission under this Public License to Share Adapted Material

permissions.commentary: CC-BY-ND-4.0 permits you to privately produce and reproduce Adapted Material (Section 2(a)(1)(B), s-58) but forbids you from Sharing it (Section 3(a)(1), s-113). So modification as a private act is permitted, but distribution of modified versions is not. Treating this as a single yes/no permission loses that nuance; 'grey' captures the split.

citations.note: Share means to provide material to the public by any means or process ... reproduction, public display, public performance, distribution
reproduce and Share the Licensed Material, in whole or in part

permissions.commentary: Distribution is permitted only for the unmodified Licensed Material. Adapted Material may not be Shared (see modification entry and s-113).

citations.note: Licensor hereby grants You a worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to exercise the Licensed Rights in the Licensed Material
produce and reproduce ... Adapted Material (private adaptation is permitted even though Sharing adaptations is not)
Patent and trademark rights are not licensed under this Public License

permissions.commentary: CC-BY-ND-4.0 expressly excludes patent and trademark rights from the license (Section 2(b)(2)). The license neither grants nor forbids patent use; patent rights remain with their holders.

citations.note: If You Share the Licensed Material, You must:
retain ... a copyright notice
retain ... a notice that refers to this Public License
indicate the Licensed Material is licensed under this Public License, and include the text of, or the URI or hyperlink to, this Public License
indicate if You modified the Licensed Material and retain an indication of any previous modifications

conditions.commentary: CC-BY-ND-4.0 has no share-alike requirement. It forbids Sharing Adapted Material entirely, so the question of what license to apply to derivatives does not arise.
